heading: Enticing desertion and harboring deserters
label: 1381 Enticing desertion and harboring deserters
text: Whoever entices or procures, or attempts or endeavors to entice or procure any person in the Armed Forces of the United States, or who has been recruited for service therein, to desert therefrom, or aids any such person in deserting or in attempting to desert from such service; orWhoever harbors, conceals, protects, or assists any such person who may have deserted from such service, knowing him to have deserted therefrom, or refuses to give up and deliver such person on the demand of any officer authorized to receive him—Shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 94 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 4235 Stat. 1097Mandatory punishment provisions were changed to alternative.Words “armed forces” were substituted for repeated references to military service, naval service, soldier and seamen.Minor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $2,000” in last par.Pub. L. 103–322
